incidents.title: KPLC Billing System Ransomware — March 2024
incidents.description: Ransomware attack on KPLC's customer billing system disrupted prepaid token generation for 48 hours, affecting 2.5 million customers. Attackers demanded KES 50 million ransom.

incidents.title: KQ Customer Data Breach — January 2024
incidents.description: Unauthorised access to Kenya Airways' frequent flyer database exposed personal data of 1.2 million customers including names, email addresses, and travel history.
responseActions: Office of the Data Protection Commissioner notified

incidents.title: KPA Port Community System Phishing Campaign
incidents.description: Targeted phishing campaign against KPA staff and port users aimed at compromising the Port Community System (PCS) used for cargo clearance.

vulnerabilities.title: SGR Train Control System — Unpatched Software
vulnerabilities.description: SGR train control and signalling system running on software versions that are 3 years out of date, with known security vulnerabilities that could allow unauthorised access to train operations.

vulnerabilities.title: SCADA System Insufficient Access Controls
vulnerabilities.description: KPLC's SCADA system for grid management has insufficient role-based access controls, allowing too many users to access critical grid control functions.

vulnerabilities.title: Pipeline SCADA Unencrypted Communications
vulnerabilities.description: Communications between KPC pipeline monitoring stations and central control use unencrypted protocols, creating risk of interception and manipulation.

controls.description: KenGen's IT systems are certified to ISO 27001 Information Security Management System standard
Dedicated SOC monitoring all IT systems for security threats and anomalies in real-time
Separation of operational technology (SCADA/grid control) networks from corporate IT networks to prevent lateral movement of threats
MFA required for all users accessing the Port Community System for cargo clearance and vessel management
